description: Inside a coffee shop, Rachel, Monica, and Joey sit on a couch around a coffee table, while Ross sits nearby in an armchair. A subtitle reading "You." appears, though no corresponding speech is audible.
Joey looks at a magazine and laughs continuously. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey's laughter continues. Rachel turns her head to look at him.
Rachel asks, "What's so funny?" Joey lowers the magazine slightly and says, "Oh, nothing. No, it's an acting exercise. I'm practicing my fake laugh." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica holds a mug and looks down at her magazine.
Monica lets out a single, abrupt "Oh," while holding her magazine. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey turns his head toward Monica with a slight smile.
Joey asks, "What? What's so funny?" as Monica lowers her gaze back to her magazine. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler walks into the coffee shop holding an unlit cigarette. Gunther steps toward him.
Gunther points at Chandler and says, "Oh, no, no."
Chandler stops walking and looks at Gunther.
Gunther points at the cigarette and says, "There's none of that in here."
Chandler moves his hand holding the cigarette and says, "Oh, come on, man."
Chandler gestures with his free hand and says, "At least let me finish this last one."
Gunther stands still, facing Chandler.
Gunther says, "Okay, but only if you give me a drag."
Chandler holds out the cigarette toward Gunther.
Chandler says, "Okay," and hands the cigarette to Gunther.
Gunther places the cigarette in his mouth and takes a drag.
Gunther tilts his head back, exhales a thick cloud of smoke upwards, and says, "Oh," with an audible sigh.
Gunther continues exhaling smoke, his eyes closed.
Gunther lowers his head, looks at the cigarette, and says, "Dark mother, once again I suckle at your smoky teeth." Audience laughter is audible.
Gunther extends his hand to pass the cigarette back to Chandler.
Chandler holds up a hand and says, "No, why don't you hold on to that one?" He then turns and walks away.
Chandler walks over to the couch and sits down next to Rachel. He places a new cigarette in his mouth.
Rachel quickly reaches out, pulls the cigarette from Chandler's mouth, and drops it onto the table. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler looks at Rachel and says, "Okay, that's like the least fun game ever." Audience laughter continues.
Rachel reaches into her bag.
Rachel holds up a small cassette tape case and says, "Well, I'm really sick of your smoking, so I brought something that is going to help you quit."
Chandler says, "Oh," while reaching toward his shoulder.
Chandler pulls a pack of cigarettes out from underneath the rolled-up sleeve of his t-shirt.
Chandler tosses the pack of cigarettes onto the table.
Chandler points at the pack and says, "No. That patch is no good." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey laughs, throwing his head back slightly, while Monica smiles and looks at Chandler.
Rachel holds the cassette tape out to Chandler and says, "It's a hypnosis tape. This woman at work used it for two weeks straight, and she hasn't smoked since." Chandler takes the tape.
Ross, sitting in the armchair, scoffs audibly and flips a page of his magazine.
Rachel turns to Ross and asks, "What's your problem?" Ross looks up and replies, "Nothing."
Ross continues, "It's just that hypnosis is beyond crap."
Rachel shifts her posture to face Ross.
Rachel says, "Ross, I watched you get hypnotized in Atlantic City."
Ross points a finger forward and says, "Hey, that guy did not hypnotize me, okay?" Overlapping him, Rachel says, "All right, because you always pull your pants down on the count..."
Rachel finishes, "...of three and play Wipeout on your butt cheeks." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross looks down slightly, then back up, shifting his posture.
Ross gestures with his hand and says, "Look, I was just trying to distract attention from that poor old woman who was trying to stuff her breasts into tube socks." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe is now visible sitting beside Chandler. She raises a hand.
Phoebe lowers her hand and says, "All right, forget hypnosis. The way to quit smoking is you have to dance naked in a field of heather and then bathe in the sweat..."
Phoebe finishes, "...of six healthy young men."
Chandler places a cigarette in his mouth and strikes a lighter.
Chandler says, "Or what my father calls Thursday night," holding the lit lighter near the cigarette. Audience laughter is audible.
The screen briefly goes black. Upbeat instrumental music begins playing as the setting changes to an exterior view of the Central Perk window, with people walking by outside.
The instrumental music fades. The setting is now inside the coffee shop, where Ross walks toward the couch carrying two mugs.
Ross extends his arm to hand one of the mugs to Rachel. She takes it and looks down at it.
Rachel holds the mug, looks at Ross, and says, "Oh, you know what? I didn't want cinnamon on this. Sorry."
Ross reaches into Rachel's mug with his fingers, pinches something, and pulls his hand out.

Ross walks away carrying his own mug. On-screen text reading "CO-EXECUTIVE PRODUCER MICHAEL BORKOW" appears. Frank Jr. enters the coffee shop and walks toward the group. Phoebe stands up with a wide smile.
Phoebe hugs Frank tightly and says, "Oh, my God. Hi." On-screen text "PRODUCED BY TODD STEVENS" appears.
Frank hugs Phoebe back. The on-screen text changes to "PRODUCER ADAM CHASE".
Phoebe releases the hug and asks, "How are you?"
Frank stands facing Phoebe.
Phoebe asks, "What are you doing here?"

Frank gestures with his hands and says, "Well, I would have called, but I lost your phone number. And then my mom locked me out of the house, so I couldn't find it. And..." On-screen text reading "PRODUCERS MICHAEL CURTIS & GREGORY S. MALINS" appears.
Frank continues, "...then I tried to find a pay phone, and the receiver was cut off." The on-screen text changes to "PRODUCER ALEXA JUNGE".
Phoebe keeps her gaze on Frank.
Phoebe asks, "What happened?"
Frank looks slightly away.
Frank looks back at Phoebe and says, "Oh, vandalism."
Phoebe maintains her focus on Frank.
Phoebe asks, "Also, what happened between you and your mom?"
Frank says, "Well, we got in a fight because she said that I was too immature to get married." On-screen text "PRODUCER IRA UNGERLEIDER" appears.
Phoebe's eyes widen.
Phoebe leans in slightly and asks, "You're getting married?" Frank nods and replies, "Oh, yeah."
Frank maintains his nod as Phoebe looks at him.
In a bustling coffee shop, Phoebe stands near a couch where Rachel, Monica, Joey, and Ross are seated. She excitedly grabs Frank by the arms.
Phoebe jumps slightly, turning to the group, and says, "My little brother's getting married." On-screen text reads "WRITTEN BY SETH KURLAND".
Frank smiles broadly and shifts his weight. The text "DIRECTED BY ROBBY BENSON" appears on screen.
Frank gestures with his hands and says, "Oh, I knew you'd be so cool about this. All right. Hey, do you want to meet her?" Phoebe replies, "Do I?"
Frank maintains his smile, looking closely at Phoebe.
Frank asks, "Do you?"
Phoebe nods emphatically, her eyebrows raised.
Phoebe smiles and says, "Yeah, I do."
Frank says, "Okay, cool. All right. She's just parking the truck," pointing a thumb toward the door. He turns away from Phoebe.
Frank walks toward the door, saying, "I'm going to go get my fiance. Man," as he exits the frame. Phoebe watches him go.
Chandler sits at a nearby table holding a wallet and looks toward the door.
Chandler says, "I would have bet good money that he'd be the first one of us to get married," while closing his wallet.
Phoebe smiles brightly, looking at the group.
Phoebe sits on the arm of the couch and says, "Yeah, isn't it fantastic?"
Monica, holding a cup, says, "Yeah, but, Phoebe, don't you think he's a little young to get married?" Phoebe replies, "Well, he's 18."
Ross, seated in an armchair, nods and says, "Exactly. It'll be illegal for him to drink at his own bachelor party."
Joey leans forward on the couch, gesturing with one hand, and adds, "Yeah, or to get a hooker."
Chandler looks toward Joey with a flat expression.
Chandler reaches for a small pitcher on his table and says, "Always illegal, Joe."
Frank walks back into the coffee shop and says, "Hey."
Frank walks forward with an older woman beside him. Phoebe stands up from the arm of the couch.
Frank puts an arm around the woman's shoulders and says, "This is my fiance, Mrs. Knight." The woman, Alice, smiles.
Phoebe stares, her eyes wide and jaw slightly dropped. Frank gestures toward her while Alice smiles.
Alice steps slightly forward and says, "You know, it's funny. Frank's told me so much about you, but you're not how I pictured you at all."
Phoebe stands stiffly, keeping her eyes wide.
Phoebe forces a tight smile and replies, "Yeah, I'm a big surprise."
Ross stands up and gestures toward his armchair. Frank and Alice move toward the chair as Phoebe sits on the arm of the couch.
Phoebe asks, "So how did you guys meet?"
Frank, sitting on the arm of the chair beside Alice, says, "Well, I was in Mrs. Knight's I mean, Alice. Sorry, Alice. I always do that." He gestures with one hand.
Frank looks down briefly, then turns back toward the group.
Frank continues, "I was in her home act class," and Alice adds, "and he was my best student." Frank smiles, looking at Alice, and says, "Yes, she was my best teacher."
Frank and Alice embrace and begin kissing passionately. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel and Monica look toward the couple with wide eyes and hands near their faces. Ross stands behind the couch, his arms crossed, watching them.
Chandler, watching from his table, says, "If that doesn't keep kids in school, what will?"
Frank and Alice stop kissing and look toward the group, smiling. Ross shifts slightly, his arms still crossed.
Ross asks, "And so now you guys are going to be married?"
Alice nods and says, "Yeah, you know, we talked about just living together, but," gesturing lightly.
Frank leans his head close to Alice's and smiles.
Alice starts, and Frank joins her in saying, "we want to have kids right away."
Phoebe's jaw drops again, her eyes wide.
Rachel, looking wide-eyed at Monica, says softly, "Oh, my God." Monica holds her hands pressed against her mouth.
Frank and Alice laugh heartily.
Phoebe forces a smile and says, "Great."
Phoebe swallows, her expression tense as she looks forward.
Phoebe whispers, "Wow," then asks, "Kids. Frank, are you sure you're ready for that?" Frank shrugs and replies, "How hard can it?"
Frank gestures with his hand, looking at Phoebe.
Frank says, "You know, who doesn't want?"
Frank puts his arm back around Alice.
Frank continues, "Besides, you know, I never had a dad around, and now I always will because it'll be me," pointing to his chest.
Alice laughs affectionately and leans against Frank. Phoebe stares at them with a strained expression.
Alice gestures with one hand and says, "Really? We do realize that there's an age difference between us." Phoebe, holding up her hands, quickly replies, "Oh, good."
Phoebe continues, "Okay, because you were acting like you didn't," but Alice interrupts, saying, "oh, no. But when it comes to love, what does age matter?"
Alice turns to Frank, and they smile at each other.
Frank and Alice embrace and begin kissing deeply again as audience laughter grows louder. The subtitle line "Shall" is not audibly spoken.
The setting changes to a city street at night, then to a dark bedroom where Chandler lies in bed with his eyes closed. A man's deep, steady voice plays from a cassette tape.
The voice on the tape says, "deeper, deeper, deeper. You are now completely asleep. You don't need to"
The voice continues, "smoke. Cigarettes don't control you," as Chandler remains still.
Chandler breathes steadily, appearing asleep.
The deep voice states, "You are a strong, confident woman."
Chandler's hands instinctively pull the blanket up tighter around his chest.
The voice repeats, "A strong, confident woman," while Chandler's face remains peaceful.
The setting changes to the exterior of the Moondance Diner during the day, then to the interior where Monica, wearing a uniform, stands behind the counter.
Monica looks at Rachel, who sits at the counter, and says, "And you know what I just realized?"
Monica leans on the counter and says rapidly, "In the last year, I've only gone out with two guys, Richard and Julio. You gotta help me out here. You gotta set me up. You gotta"
Monica finishes, "get me back in the game." Rachel pours a drink and replies, "Well, that shouldn't be a problem. I mean, I work in fashion, and all I meet are eligible straight men."
Rachel looks down with a resigned expression as audience laughter is heard.
In a diner, Pete sits at the counter holding a pen over some papers. Across the counter stands Monica, wearing a blonde wig and a pink uniform.
Pete looks up at Monica and says, "You can get you something else. Yeah, I'll have a slice of cheesecake and a date, if you're giving them out."
Monica picks up a plate holding a slice of cheesecake.

Monica sets the plate in front of Pete and says, "Haven't you and I covered that topic?" Pete replies, "Come on." Monica gestures toward herself and adds, "you just said to her that you only want to go out with me because my blonde"
Monica crosses her arms as Pete looks at her.
Monica continues, "wig and the big boobs and the fact that I serve you food."
Pete smiles, touching his neck, and replies, "Well, if that were true, I'd be dating my Aunt Ruth,"
Audience laughter is heard. Monica turns to the counter behind her, smiling slightly.
Pete continues, "and the two times we went out, it was just plain awkward."
Audience laughter continues. Monica turns back around, laughing. Rachel now stands at the counter next to Pete.
Pete gestures toward Monica while looking at Rachel and asks, "Come on, you think she should go out with me?"
Rachel shakes her head slightly and replies, "Don't."
Rachel hesitates, holding a mug near her mouth. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel says, "I mean, are you sure you want to go out with her? I mean, that ain't a pretty picture in the morning."
Rachel lowers her mug and gestures with her right hand, adding, "You know what I mean? That wig"
Rachel makes tossing motions near her chest and continues, "all in disarray and boobs flung over the nightstand." Audience laughter is heard.
Pete laughs and points his pen toward Monica.
Pete says, "Really?" Rachel nods, saying, "Think about it." Pete adds, "I will."
The setting changes to an apartment living room. Joey and Ross sit in black reclining armchairs. Behind them, Phoebe paces.
Phoebe stops pacing and says, "No, I know. I know that this is Frank's life."
Phoebe walks around the armchairs to stand in front of Joey and Ross.
Phoebe raises her hands and says, "I don't want to be all judgmental, but this is sick, it's sick and wrong."
Joey turns his head to look at Phoebe.
Joey looks at Phoebe and asks, "Jeez. What is it, the age?"
Phoebe gestures widely and replies, "No, no, I'm fine with the age, you know, until it starts sticking its tongue down my little brother's throat." Audience laughter follows.
Joey leans back in his chair while Ross keeps his gaze on Phoebe.
Joey gestures with an open hand and says, "Pheebs, he seems to enjoy it,"
Phoebe points toward Joey and says, "but I mean, do you think he's going to enjoy it when he's up to his elbows in the diapers of all the"
Phoebe waves her hands near her head and continues, "babies they have to have right away?"
Phoebe turns and takes a few steps back toward the chairs.
Phoebe walks past the armchairs, saying, "This is not fair to Frank, and it's not fair to the babies."
Phoebe stops, turns back to Joey and Ross with her hands raised, and says, "And you know what? It's not good home economics."
Joey gestures toward Phoebe with both hands.
Joey asks, "Have you told them how you feel?" Phoebe replies, "Yes."
Phoebe lowers her gaze and adds softly, "Not out loud."
Audience laughter is audible as Ross looks at Phoebe.
Ross says, "Pheebs, he's not a mind reader."
Phoebe turns her head slightly to the side.
Phoebe, looking off to the side, says, "We don't know that."
Audience laughter is audible.
Ross says, "Pheebs, if you don't tell him, soon he's going to be married, and then you're going to hate yourself." Phoebe begins to reply, "Yeah, but"
Phoebe points to her chest and continues, "if I do tell him, then he's going to hate myself."
Phoebe gestures toward the hallway with her right hand.
Phoebe says, "Look at him and his mom. I can't."
Phoebe waves her hands in front of her.
Phoebe leans forward slightly and says, "But you guys can. Please, you got to talk him out of it."
Ross and Joey simultaneously answer, "No."
Phoebe quickly steps behind Ross's chair and grabs the headrest.
Phoebe shakes Ross's chair and says, "Come on, you guys."
Phoebe releases the chair, steps back to the front, and gestures with both open hands.
Phoebe says, "You have nothing to lose. I have everything to lose."
Phoebe gestures emphatically and continues, "Do you want me to lose everything? Everything?"
Ross and Joey look at Phoebe, then lower their gazes. Joey looks up slightly.
Joey says, "No."
Phoebe lowers her hands and nods.
Phoebe says, "Okay. I'm going to go get Frank."
Phoebe turns, grabs a green jacket, and exits the apartment. Joey leans toward Ross.
Joey looks at Ross, gesturing with one hand, and says, "So we're walking down the street, and I turn to you and I say,"
Joey smiles slightly and continues, "hey, let's go hang out at Totally Nude Nudes, remember?"
Joey points at Ross and says, "And then you turn to me and you say, man, let's just hang out at your place. Well, that was a nice move, dumbass." Joey playfully slaps Ross's shoulder.
The setting changes to a different apartment. Rachel opens the front door and walks in, followed by Monica.
Rachel takes off her coat and says, "I think you should definitely go out with this guy." Monica shuts the door and replies, "He just doesn't do anything for me."
Rachel turns to Monica and asks, "Monica last Saturday night. What happened on Walker, Texas Ranger?"
Monica looks up, tilting her head thoughtfully.
Monica raises her hands to chest level and says, "Well, Walker was looking for this big busload of kids."
Rachel smiles at Monica.
Rachel says, "All right, I get your"
Rachel turns her head away, and Monica lowers her hands.
In an apartment kitchen, Chandler walks toward the camera holding a black briefcase.
Chandler says, "You know, I forgot the combination of this about a year ago. I just carry it around," stepping toward Monica and Rachel, who stand near a table.
Chandler places the briefcase on a chair and pats his coat pockets.
"Any chapsticks?" Chandler asks.
Monica searches her purse and says, "Yeah." Rachel looks at Chandler and asks, "Hey, how are those tapes working out for you?"
Chandler nods at Rachel.
"You know what? Pretty good," Chandler says. "Good," Rachel replies.

"I haven't smoked yet today," Chandler says, gesturing with his hands. "I feel great and confident." He places a hand on his chest and looks at Monica, adding, "That is a stunning blouse," his pitch lowering.
Monica and Rachel look at each other, their eyebrows furrowed.
"Thank you," Monica says. Rachel touches her own chest.
Monica hands Chandler a small tube, and he begins applying it to his lips.
"Come on, let's give Pete a chance," Rachel says to Monica. "Come on. He was funny."
"He seems really nice, and that check thing was adorable," Rachel adds. Chandler stops applying the item and asks, "What check thing?"
Monica turns toward Chandler.
"As a joke, this customer at work who has a crush on me gave me a $20,000 tip," Monica says, handing Chandler a slip of paper.
"His numbers on the check. He just did it. So I'd call," Monica says. Chandler studies the paper and says, "Pete Becker."
"Pete?" Monica asks.
Chandler holds the paper, looking down at it, then steps toward his briefcase.
He pulls a magazine from the briefcase, opens it, and flips through the pages.
Chandler points at a page and asks, "Is this him?" Monica looks at it and says, "That's Bill Clinton."
Chandler lowers the magazine slightly, examining the page closer.
"Who's he hugging?" Chandler asks.
Monica and Rachel lean in to look at the magazine.
"Oh, my God. That's Pete. Why is Bill hugging Pete?" Monica asks, her gaze on the magazine.
Chandler says, "This guy invented Moss 865. Every office in the world," pointing at the page.
"...uses that program," Chandler concludes. "We use it," Rachel says. "There you go," Chandler replies, gesturing with his hand.
Rachel gasps, bringing her hands up to her collarbone.
"Oh, my God," Rachel says.
"Monica's going to go out with a millionaire," Rachel adds, smiling.
"No, I'm not going to go out with him," Monica says. "Why not? He is a millionaire," Rachel asks.
Monica raises her hands, palms open.
"So?" Monica asks. Rachel reaches for the check.
Rachel takes the check from Chandler and says, "Oh, my God. I can't believe this is a real $20,000 check. This is just so exciting."
Rachel smiles at the check.
"Or incredibly offensive," Chandler says.
Rachel lowers the check, her smile fading.
"Well, yeah, sure, that, too," Rachel says, her pitch flat.
Chandler resumes applying the small tube to his lips, closing his mouth over it.
His eyes widen as he realizes he has taken a bite, and he quickly grabs a napkin from the counter.
Chandler spits the item into the napkin and wipes his mouth, holding the napkin against his face.
"What?" Rachel asks, looking at Chandler.
The setting changes to a different apartment. Ross and Joey stand near a recliner where Frank Jr. sits.
"All we're saying is, don't rush into anything," Ross says, gesturing with his hands.
"Yeah, come on, think about it. You're 18, okay? She's" Joey adds, leaning forward.
"...44. When you're 36, she's going to be 88," Joey finishes, pointing at Frank Jr.
Frank Jr. looks up at Joey, gesturing with his hands.
"You don't think I know that?" Frank Jr. asks.
Ross and Joey exchange glances.
Chandler walks into the room wearing sweatpants and holding a basketball. "All right, I'm going to go play basketball," he says.
"Or at least let the really good guys use my ball while I say, Can I play?" he continues, stopping near the door.
"Can I play?" Chandler repeats, his pitch rising.
Chandler turns and pulls the door open to leave.
"Oh, Joe," Chandler says, pausing at the open door.
Chandler exits the apartment, pulling the door shut behind him.
The screen turns black.
